Title: OsPK2 encodes a plastidic pyruvate kinase involved in rice endosperm starch synthesis, compound granule formation and grain filling

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major energy storage reserve in seeds; supports grain filling and seed vigor
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes endosperm starch accumulation, normal starch compound granule formation, grain filling and seed storability; loss or reduction causes chalky white-core endosperm, reduced starch and altered starch properties.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plastidic pyruvate kinase alpha-subunit type; PK domain
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively expressed in all tested organs; higher in stems, panicles and leaves, lower in roots and developing grains
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in root, stem, leaf, sheath, panicle, glume, spikelet and developing grains
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native OsPK2 promoter (constitutive pattern in GUS assay); maize UBIQUITIN1 promoter and rice UBIQUITIN1 promoter used for transgene expression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutation is C-to-T in the 4th exon causing Ser296Leu in a highly conserved residue; mutation reduces pyruvate content and causes broad metabolic disturbances including altered fatty acid, protein and starch-related gene expression. Although OsPK2 is an enzyme, its central role here also includes heteromer formation and broad metabolic coordination.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; complementation with genomic clone; overexpression rescue; RNAi knockdown; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; recombinant protein enzyme assay; endogenous PK activity assay; pyruvate measurement; subcellular localization; SEM/TEM and starch physicochemical profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPK2 was identified by map-based cloning as the causal gene for the ospk2 white-core, poor grain-filling mutant. 2. OsPK2 encodes a plastidic pyruvate kinase with reduced activity in the Ser296Leu mutant. 3. Genetic rescue restored normal grain phenotype, while RNAi and CRISPR disruption reproduced chalky, low-starch grains. 4. OsPK2 is required for normal starch compound granule formation, starch content, amylose content and grain weight. 5. Mutation also increased seed lipid and protein content, altered amylopectin chain distribution and reduced seed germination after storage.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes reduced grain weight and yield per plant, slower grain filling, altered starch quality, increased lipid and protein content, and reduced germination after storage.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural field conditions; grain developmental stages 3-20 DAF were critical for phenotype assessment; 1-year seed storage revealed reduced germination in mutant.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving grain filling, yield and grain quality in rice; maintaining or enhancing OsPK2 function could support starch accumulation and endosperm appearance, but perturbation may shift carbon flux toward lipids and compromise storability.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Glycolysis linked to plastidial carbon supply for starch and fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Plastidic pyruvate-producing step supplying carbon/energy for endosperm starch synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Irreversibly transfers phosphate from phosphoenolpyruvate to ADP to form pyruvate and ATP
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and energy for plastidial starch biosynthesis; also affects competing fatty acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream glycolytic step; terminal step of glycolysis feeding plastid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control enzyme in plastid glycolysis affecting carbon flux into starch and fatty acid metabolism; explicit rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plastidic pyruvate kinase alpha-subunit type; PK catalytic domain
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutive; highest in stems, panicles and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutive across organs including developing grains
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native OsPK2 promoter; UBIQUITIN1 promoter for overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes starch accumulation and proper starch granule architecture in endosperm; reduced enzyme activity lowers pyruvate and starch while increasing lipid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; complementation; overexpression; RNAi; CRISPR/Cas9; recombinant enzyme assay; endogenous PK assay; pyruvate assay; localization; Y2H/BiFC; metabolite and starch property analyses
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPK2 encodes a plastid-localized pyruvate kinase catalyzing the final step of glycolysis. 2. The Ser296Leu mutation significantly reduces PK activity in recombinant protein and plant tissues. 3. Reduced OsPK2 activity decreases pyruvate content and disrupts expression of starch-, pyruvate- and fatty-acid-related genes. 4. OsPK2 function is necessary for normal endosperm starch synthesis, compound granule formation and grain filling.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation causes lower grain yield/weight, chalkiness, altered starch pasting and gelatinization properties, and reduced seed storability.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Grain developmental stage, especially 10-12 DAF, is important for biochemical interpretation; 1-year storage affects germination phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed carbon reserve for grain filling and germination
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to improve starch-rich grain quality and yield; should be preserved or enhanced rather than disrupted to avoid carbon flux imbalance toward lipids.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The first 97 N-terminal amino acids are necessary for plastid targeting. OsPK2 can form heteromeric complexes with other plastidic PK subunits, suggesting enzyme activity may depend on multimer composition.
